failure_modes.text: A plan can look inevitable only because the current story hides other valid lenses and success criteria.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when the first story is trapping the decision.
failure_modes.mitigation: After reframing, check whether the new lens opened genuinely different options or merely relabeled the same plan: if the action set didn't change, the reframe was decorative.

failure_modes.text: Reframing can become euphemism when teams rename a hard reality without improving the truth surface or option set.
failure_modes.mitigation: Demand truth-surface improvement: accept a reframe only if it makes previously hidden trade-offs, risks, or stakeholder concerns more visible, not less.

failure_modes.text: New language can turn into avoidance when leaders keep resetting the conversation instead of specifying the next decision test.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when reframing keeps resetting the conversation instead of forcing a decision test.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pair any new language with a concrete decision test: specify what evidence would confirm or disconfirm the reframed hypothesis so the conversation cannot keep resetting indefinitely.

premortem_questions.text: What different action becomes reasonable if we look at the same facts through a user, system, risk, or trade-off lens?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Works when the same facts support different actions depending on viewpoint.

premortem_questions.text: Which binary in our language is fake, and what conditional or segmented option is hidden by it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Works when the team is stuck in a false binary.

premortem_questions.text: What evidence or trade-off would actually make this new frame better instead of merely nicer sounding?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when reframing keeps resetting the conversation instead of forcing a decision test.

heuristics.text: Change the question before changing the answer when the original framing is making one path look falsely inevitable.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when the first recommendation sounds inevitable because of how the problem was posed.

heuristics.text: Try alternative lenses that change what counts as success, risk, or reversibility.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when leaders need a deliberate frame shift.

heuristics.text: Use reframing only when it sharpens evidence, trade-offs, or decision quality rather than softening reality.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the chunks close to false-binary escape, success-criterion shifts, and anti-euphemism discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor narrative-sprawl because the raw source already supports compact reframing chunks.
open_questions: The source also supports communication and emotional processing, but the Wave 2 surface stays on decision quality.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on systems-thinking and peer challenge.
antagonists: The source is strongest against commitment pressure and bias-blind-spot style egocentric framing.
structured_tensions: The main tensions are around false binaries, euphemism, and frame-resetting without decision pressure.
